novel_features.name: Two-stage funnel: seed → deep research
novel_features.description: Seed candidates from Google Places, then deep-research each against locale-specific review sites, forums, and search engines — return only the 3-5 amazing matches.
novel_features.rationale: No existing tool does Stage-1 (real geo-search) → Stage-2 (locale-aware criteria research) → Stage-3 (trust-weighted rank with closed-signal kill-gate). v1 fanned out blindly; v2 inverts to a funnel.
novel_features.example: wanderlust-goat-pp-cli near "Park Hyatt Tokyo" --criteria "vintage jazz kissaten with no tourists" --identity "coffee snob into 70s Japanese kissaten culture" --minutes 15
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user asks for an opinionated walking-distance recommendation matching stated identity + criteria, this is the single command to invoke. Returns evidence-cited picks, not a 40-row dump.

novel_features.name: Heuristic GOAT (no LLM)
novel_features.description: Same two-stage funnel as `near` but explicitly no-LLM — keyword-overlap criteria match runs locally, free.
novel_features.rationale: Identity-aware ranking shouldn't require an API key. The heuristic path uses static criteria→source mappings + evidence-keyword overlap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick `goat` when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is absent or you want determinism. Same shape as `near`, no external LLM calls.

novel_features.name: Closed-signal kill-gate
novel_features.description: Explicit operational/closed lookup across every Stage-2 source for one place — surfaces conflicting signals and recent RIP keywords.
novel_features.rationale: Restaurant/cafe data is famously stale. Google business_status alone misses 'permanently closed' signals that show up in Tabelog headers, Naver status, Le Fooding deletions, OSM disused: tags, and recent Reddit threads.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When deciding whether to recommend a place to a user, run this first. Conflicting signals are explicit, not silently averaged.

novel_features.name: Walking-time radius (not crow-flies)
novel_features.description: Radius is computed as walking minutes × 4.5 km/h ÷ 1.3 tortuosity, not haversine meters — matches the actual experience of walking a city grid.
novel_features.rationale: A 500m crow-flies radius in Tokyo is 800-900m of actual walking. A user asking for '15-minute walk' wants what they can actually reach.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user's input is time-bounded, use minutes-based commands. The CLI applies the tortuosity multiplier internally.

novel_features.name: Trust-weighted ranking
novel_features.description: Print every source that mentioned a place, the trust weight, country boost, walking time, criteria match, and the final goat-score breakdown.
novel_features.rationale: The score is auditable. Google base + Tabelog/Naver +0.10 in-country + Wikipedia notability +0.05 + Reddit (≥10 upvotes, ≥3 comments) +0.05 — every contribution is reproducible.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user disagrees with a ranking, run why to see the score components. When the user wants to share evidence, the same output is the citation list.

novel_features.name: Country → research-stage map (data table)
novel_features.description: Single struct slice in internal/regions/regions.go drives every regional dispatch decision. Adding a country = adding one row.
novel_features.rationale: v1 scattered switch statements across the orchestrator and per-source clients. v2 has one source of truth. The wiring test asserts every source in internal/<source>/ is invoked from cli/, so 'scaffolded but never wired' regressions are impossible.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user asks 'why did I get a JP source for a KR query?' or 'which sources cover Italy?', `coverage` shows the active region row for the synced city.

novel_features.name: Agent research-plan emitter
novel_features.description: Output a typed JSON query plan agents execute in a loop — country-aware, ordered by trust, ready to fan out to MCP/agent loops.
novel_features.rationale: Frontier-model agents already have web-search tools. Give them the right plan instead of the answer; they fan out and dedupe themselves.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the agent has its own search tools, this is the right surface — emit the plan, let the agent loop. Don't burn tokens on Stage-2 if the caller has better tooling.

novel_features.name: Crossover walks
novel_features.description: Restaurants near (within 200m of) Wikipedia-notable historic sites or Atlas Obscura entries — food + culture in one walk.
novel_features.rationale: The intersection of editorial food and notable culture is a richer signal than either alone. Local SQLite join finds it free; no API has this surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user wants the trip to feel like more than just lunch, crossover finds the food/culture pairings within walking distance.

novel_features.name: Golden-hour viewpoint hunt
novel_features.description: Compute sunrise/sunset/blue-hour locally (pure Go, no API) and pair with viewpoints photographers know about within walking distance.
novel_features.rationale: Time + place + viewpoint signal isn't combined anywhere. SunCalc + OSM tourism=viewpoint + walking-radius gives the photographer's right spot at the right hour.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user is a photographer or specifically wants 'sunset spots,' golden-hour combines the three signals locally.

novel_features.name: Route-view exploration
novel_features.description: Walking polyline from A to B, then everything interesting along the path — not just at the endpoints.
novel_features.rationale: OSRM gives the route; OSM tags + editorial sources score the segments. The journey is the product.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user is walking somewhere specific, route-view layers the discovery onto the path they're already taking.

novel_features.name: City pre-trip sync
novel_features.description: Pre-cache editorial best-of, Reddit threads, Wikipedia, OSM POIs, Atlas Obscura, and every implemented Stage-2 source for offline use. Wires ALL implemented Stage-2 clients (the v1 silent-drop bug is now a wiring-test failure, not a runtime miss).
novel_features.rationale: Travel happens with bad wifi. Pre-caching the local SQLite store means the heavy commands work offline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user is about to travel, run sync-city for offline depth. Coverage report tells them what landed.

novel_features.name: Quiet-hour discovery
novel_features.description: Places that locals describe as quiet at the requested time, intersected with OSM opening hours and walking radius.
novel_features.rationale: Reddit and forum threads contain 'dead before 6, packed after 8' signals nobody surfaces. SQLite + keyword extraction makes it queryable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user wants to avoid crowds, quiet-hour pulls Reddit/forum quiet-hour signals and intersects with hours and radius.

narrative.headline: What a knowledgeable local with great taste would tell you to walk to from here.
narrative.value_prop: Two-stage funnel: seed candidates from Google Places, then deep-research each against locale-aware sources (Tabelog/Naver/Le Fooding for the country you're in), trust-weight by source authority, kill-gate anything that's permanently closed, and return the 3-5 amazing things — not the comprehensive 40-row dump.
narrative.auth_narrative: GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY is required for live Stage-1 seeding. Get a key at https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/places/web-service/get-api-key (free $200/mo credit). ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is optional and enables sharper criteria judgment via --llm; without it, the free heuristic path runs.

quickstart.comment: verify Nominatim/OSRM reachable and required env vars set

quickstart.command: wanderlust-goat-pp-cli near "Park Hyatt Tokyo" --criteria "vintage jazz kissaten with no tourists" --identity "coffee snob into 70s Japanese kissaten culture" --minutes 15
quickstart.comment: the headline workflow: identity + criteria + walking radius

quickstart.command: wanderlust-goat-pp-cli goat 35.6895,139.6917 --criteria "high-end seafood with counter seating" --minutes 12
quickstart.comment: no-LLM path; same shape, free, deterministic
audit a ranking — every source, weight, and contribution
is this place still open? cross-source verdict

troubleshoots.symptom: exit code 4: Google Places auth missing
near returns 0 results in country X
troubleshoots.fix: country X may not have a Stage-2 region row yet — run `coverage <city> --json` to see the active region; add a row to internal/regions/regions.go to extend

troubleshoots.symptom: Atlas Obscura returns nothing for my city
troubleshoots.fix: Atlas Obscura's slug index doesn't cover every city; this is expected, the dispatcher falls through silently

troubleshoots.symptom: Tabelog/Naver/Le Fooding returns nothing for a place that exists
troubleshoots.fix: Stage-2 lookup is by name; the source's anti-bot mitigation may have intercepted. Run with --verbose to see per-source HTTP status; rate-limit backoff is per-source

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for wanderlust-goat when an agent or user wants opinionated walking-distance recommendations matching stated identity and criteria — not a comprehensive list. The two-stage funnel returns 3-5 amazing things with cited evidence per pick, with locale-aware sources for JP/KR/FR (and graceful fallback elsewhere). Closed-signal kill-gate means stale results don't ship.

recipes.title: Find vintage kissaten near a Tokyo hotel
recipes.command: wanderlust-goat-pp-cli near "Park Hyatt Tokyo" --criteria "vintage 50-year-old kissaten with award-winning pour-over, no tourists" --identity "coffee snob into 70s Japanese kissaten culture" --minutes 15 --json --select results.name,results.evidence,results.walking_minutes
recipes.explanation: Identity + criteria + radius produces a ranked, evidence-cited shortlist; --select narrows the deeply nested response.

recipes.title: Audit why a place ranked
recipes.explanation: Every source, trust weight, country boost, walking-time, and criteria-match score in one breakdown.

recipes.title: Confirm operational status
recipes.explanation: Conflicting closed signals (Google operational, Tabelog 閉店, recent Reddit RIP keywords) are surfaced explicitly.

recipes.title: Pre-cache a city for offline trip use
recipes.explanation: sync-city pulls every implemented Stage-2 source plus shared sources into the local store; coverage shows what landed.

recipes.title: Hand off the plan to an agent loop
recipes.explanation: When the caller has its own web-search tooling, emit the plan instead of running it; the JSON is country-aware and trust-ordered.

trigger_phrases: what should I walk to from here
